name: vector-racing
author: cristian-milea
description: Turn-based vector racing. 9-way pad on the phone, circuit on the e-ink.
long_description: Pen-and-paper Racetrack on the e-ink. Each turn you nudge your velocity by ±1 in x/y; momentum carries you, so brake before the corners or clip the wall. Procedurally generated closed circuits in nine flavours — oval, triangle, square, pentagon, hexagon, zigzag, scalloped, kidney, and self-crossing figure-8 — as a daily-seeded track everyone shares, or random. Pick Safe (stop on a crash) or Hardcore (back to the line) before the off, run 1–3 laps, and chase the computed optimum.
